title: Podman does not tolerate special characters in proxy credentials
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** When running podman behind the proxy with authentication, the podman does not tolerate special characters in proxy password. Not sure if this is a but or the behavior is by design, but I did not find any warning that proxy password should not contain any special characters. It seems however, that numbers can be used. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Establish a proxy with authentication with username and password where password would include special characters 2. Set the HTTP_PROXY variable `HTTP_PROXY=http://cda:ICPassw0rd#@192.168.10.10:3128` 3. run the podman login command **Describe the results you received:** in case of the following HTTP_PROXY setting (mind the # in the password): `HTTP_PROXY=http://cda:ICPassw0rd#@192.168.10.10:3128` the podman login would return the following message: ``` root@cdautil:proxytests $ podman login docker.io -u bodemo Password: Error: error authenticating creds for "docker.io": error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup http on 192.168.10.2:53: no such host ``` if proxy password without special characters is used the podman works well `HTTP_PROXY=http://cda:ICPassw0rd@192.168.10.10:3128` ``` root@cdautil:proxytests $ podman login docker.io -u bodemo Password: Login Succeeded! ``` In contrast curl command with proxy password with special characters in the env. variable or in command like works ok `HTTP_PROXY=http://cda:ICPassw0rd#@192.168.10.10:3128` ``` root@cdautil:proxytests $ curl --proxy http://cda:ICPassw0rd#@192.168.10.10:3128 https://google.com 301 Moved

301 Moved

The document has moved here. ``` **Describe the results you expected:** **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** **Output of `podman version`:** ``` root@cdautil:upi-bmce $ podman version Version: 3.0.2-dev API Version: 3.0.0 Go Version: go1.15.7 Built: Wed Apr 7 10:36:54 2021 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` root@cdautil:upi-bmce $ podman info --debug host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.19.8 cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: conmon-2.0.26-1.module+el8.4.0+10607+f4da7515.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.26, commit: b883692702312720058141f16b6002ab26ead2e7' cpus: 4 distribution: distribution: '"rhel"' version: "8.4" eventLogger: file hostname: cdautil.cda.internal idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 4.18.0-305.el8.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic memFree: 147111936 memTotal: 8145596416 ociRuntime: name: runc package: runc-1.0.0-70.rc92.module+el8.4.0+10607+f4da7515.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/runc version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.2-dev' os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true selinuxEnabled: true slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 3101184000 swapTotal: 3439325184 uptime: 74h 40m 8.29s (Approximately 3.08 days) registries: search: - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.redhat.io - docker.io store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "true" imageStore: number: 1 runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.0.0 Built: 1617784614 BuiltTime: Wed Apr 7 10:36:54 2021 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.15.7 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.0.2-dev ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` root@cdautil:upi-bmce $ rpm -q podman podman-3.0.1-6.module+el8.4.0+10607+f4da7515.x86_64 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** Yes, I checked Podman Troubleshooting Guide. Not sure I checked the latest version of Podman. **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** the podbam is running on RHEL 8 in vmware env.

comments.body: I don't believe Podman does anything with this field, but passes it to Golang, which might be screwing up.
Does it work when you urlencode the password?

in case I encode the `http://cda:ICPassw0rd#@192.168.10.10:3128` to `http%3A%2F%2Fcda%3AICPassw0rd%23%40192.168.10.10%3A3128` 

the error is still there
```
root@cdautil:proxytests $ podman login docker.io -u bodemo
Password: 
Error: error authenticating creds for "docker.io": error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp :0: connect: connection refused
```
You should only urlencode the password

yes, I just tried and in this case it works
`HTTPS_PROXY=http://cda:ICPassw0rd%23@192.168.10.10:3128`

```
root@cdautil:proxytests $ podman login docker.io -u bodemo
Password: 
Login Succeeded!
```
Since Podman can not fix this, I am going to close. You could open an issue with golang.  Glad you have a fix.
